sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Judy Gomez, is a 61-year-old woman with ongoing management of psoriatic arthritis.
She reports being on a decreased dose of prednisone and has been experiencing occasional pain in her feet, particularly after days off from work where she is moving around more.
She mentions taking an extra dose of prednisone once a week due to increased pain.
She also reports feeling horrible after taking methotrexate with the COVID-19 vaccine, but noted an improvement after taking it again a week later.
She expresses concern about bruising on her skin, particularly after minor bumps.
She has been experiencing bruising on her skin without feeling pain.
She has a history of psoriasis, which is causing ridges and nail changes.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of psoriatic arthritis, and is currently on prednisone and methotrexate.
Review of systems: The patient reports occasional pain in her feet and joints, but notes an improvement after increasing the methotrexate dose.
